Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The player moved left. Let me check the new position and see if the blue square (node 7) changed color or anything interesting happened. I should also try to navigate to all three blue squares and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Player position:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check all the blue-framed squares
print("\nBlue-framed squares:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'c' or (n['pixels'] == 12 and n['children']):
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")

# Check the central blue square
for n in nodes:
    if n['id'] == 7:
        print(f"\nCenter blue square: id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The player shape changed. Before, the white square was a 2x2 (4 pixels) and the green was a 3x4 (12 pixels). Now:
- White (id=9): boundary=[[40, 28], [43, 28]] - this looks like a 4x1 vertical strip (4 pixels)
- Green (id=10): boundary=[[40, 29], [40, 31], [43, 31], [43, 29]] - this is a 4x3 horizontal area (12 pixels)
Wait, let me re-read the boundaries. The white square boundary is [[40, 28], [43, 28]] which is only 2 points - that's weird for a 4-pixel object. It seems like the player rotated! The white part is now a vertical strip on the left side, and the green part is to the right of it.
Actually, looking at the image, the player now looks like it's facing left - the white square is on the left edge and the green body extends to the right. So the player rotates to face the direction of movement!
Let me think about the goal. We have 3 blue-framed squares arranged in a triangle. Maybe the goal is to visit all of them, or to collect something. Let me try pressing SPACE near one of them, or try navigating to each one.
Actually, let me reconsider. The player moved from col 32-35 to col 28-31 (moved left by 4). The player is now at col 28-31, row 40-43. The left blue square is at col 16-19, row 28-31. I need to move left more and up to reach it.
Let me try to navigate to the left blue square first.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player rotates to face movement direction. After moving LEFT, player is now at row 40-43, col 28-31 (white head on left, green body extending right). The 3 blue-framed squares remain at: left (row 28-31, col 16-19), right (row 24-27, col 44-47), center-below (row 36-39, col 32-35). Player can't pass through blue squares.
Goal model: Need to visit/interact with the blue-framed squares. May need to navigate to each one.
Plan: Navigate to the left blue square first (move LEFT twice more, then UP), then explore what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Navigate to the left blue square
# Player at col 28-31, need to get to col 16-19 (2 more LEFT moves)
# Player at row 40-43, need to get to row 28-31 (need to go up)
# But first let's go left to align with the left square
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
